title: ArPhase
description: One logical phase of an Aristotelian mythos — beginning, middle, or end — per design/aristotelian-sketch-01.md A2. Phases are logical divisions connected by necessity or probability (Poetics 1451a), not equal-page-target beats. `scope_event_ids` names the substrate events authoring this phase; the A7 unity-of-action check enforces disjoint, covering phase scopes at the mythos level (not at the per-phase record level, which is this schema's jurisdiction).

id.description: Stable identifier for this phase (e.g., 'ph_beginning'). No prefix convention enforced — the 'ph_' convention is descriptive only. PFS6-P2.

role.description: Which of the three Aristotelian logical divisions this phase occupies. Closed enum per A2 — additional role names would be a dialect amendment, not a record-shape authoring choice. PFS6-P3.

scope_event_ids.description: Substrate event ids comprising this phase. Empty array is legal (a phase may exist as scaffolding before events are authored; A7's self-verifier surfaces this). Plain-string references per PFS6-X2 / PFS5 OQ3 — not $ref-typed to event.json. Cross-reference consistency is a verifier/test-layer audit surface, not a record-shape concern. PFS6-P4.

annotation.description: Optional author prose annotation. Python default is an empty string; schema admits the empty string and absence interchangeably. PFS6-P5.

min_event_count.description: A15-SE1 (aristotelian-sketch-04). Optional hard cardinality lower bound on scope_event_ids for the compiler. 0 (default) means unbounded floor. A7.12 enforces non-negative (second line of defense for Python-level negative values that bypass the schema), min <= max when both > 0, and current scope_event_ids count >= bound when active. Compiler stage 2 (feasibility) folds non-zero values into the two-level budget calculation. PFS-N9.

max_event_count.description: A15-SE1 (aristotelian-sketch-04). Optional hard cardinality upper bound on scope_event_ids for the compiler. 0 (default) means unbounded ceiling. A7.12 enforces non-negative and current count <= bound when active. PFS-N10.

pacing_preference.description: A16-SP3 (aristotelian-sketch-04 / DCS1 side 2). Soft preference for stage-4 ranker scoring. Empty string (default) is neutral. Canonical-plus-open: published canonical vocabulary {slow_burn, even, accelerating, rapid_escalation, decelerating}; non-canonical strings are admissible (the ranker handles unrecognized values as default-neutral). Per DCS3 there is NO verifier coverage for A16 fields — schema accepts any string and the open-string posture matches PFS7 role_labels / PFS9-LO3 / PFS11-AO3 / PFS12-O3 / PFS13-MR2. PFS-N11.
